step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the conclusion provided by @Agent4. The key points made about concrete fitting into the category of being both strongly compressible while having lower tensile capabilities support why "Brittle" would be correct here. One additional point could further solidify this idea—many ceramics like glass also demonstrate similar behavior patterns—they shatter easily upon impact due to their inability to stretch outwards despite taking immense inward pressures very well. Thus, reiterating my agreement:

Answer: **B: Brittleness**. Your summary encapsulates accurately why concretes' structural behaviors mirror those defined for Brittle materials; thus reinforcing our selection remains valid given all presented evidence and considerations regarding physical sciences underlying construction principles! Agreeing fully with Agent4's rationale reinforces confidence in choosing option **B: Brittleness**.
